policy: Temporary inventory for product runtime references to POC-era scripts, POC-shaped output files, and POC output paths. New runtime references must not be added unless they have an owner story and removal condition.

entries.reason_remaining: The current importer and asset serving still read existing POC-shaped output trees until saved project data becomes authoritative.
entries.removal_condition: Default product outputs move outside pocs/ and import remains an explicit migration path only.

entries.reason_remaining: A single helper localizes the remaining POC script path references so later stories can delete the dependency without hunting call sites.
entries.removal_condition: No product runtime stage calls poc_scripts_root.

entries.workflow: dependency preflight for temporary legacy runners
entries.reason_remaining: Preflight must detect missing dependencies for the temporary legacy runners before later stories replace those runners.
entries.removal_condition: Preflight checks product-owned services and adapters instead of POC-era scripts.

entries.workflow: world, storyboard, prompt, and keyframe generation
entries.reason_remaining: World, storyboard, prompt, and keyframe generation are still delegated to the legacy full-song stage runner until product-owned generation services exist.
entries.removal_condition: World, storyboard, prompt, and keyframe stages run through product-owned services and adapters.

entries.reason_remaining: Scene regeneration still shells through legacy generation/render scripts until product-owned rendering services exist.
entries.removal_condition: Scene keyframe and clip regeneration use product-owned render services and adapters.

entries.reason_remaining: Final render still invokes the legacy clip renderer until product-owned final render service exists.
entries.removal_condition: Final video rendering uses product-owned render service and adapter.

entries.reason_remaining: The importer still reads POC-shaped JSON artifacts to migrate existing projects into SQLite.
entries.removal_condition: Normal runtime no longer imports automatically from generated artifacts; artifact import is explicit migration-only behavior.

entries.workflow: rescan generated artifacts into saved data
entries.reason_remaining: Rescan still copies generated JSON artifact content into the database until saved records are authoritative for normal workflow.
entries.removal_condition: Rescan is explicit migration/refresh behavior and cannot silently override saved product edits.

entries.reason_remaining: Existing projects still store asset paths under POC-shaped output trees.
entries.removal_condition: Assets are registered as product artifacts independent of POC output roots.

entries.workflow: scene fields that mirror legacy renderer expectations
entries.reason_remaining: Some scene metadata names still mirror the legacy renderer until product render contracts are introduced.
entries.removal_condition: Scene render contracts are product-owned and no comments or code depend on legacy renderer naming.

entries.reason_remaining: Stage orchestration still describes legacy runner behavior until centralized workflow state and product services replace it.
entries.removal_condition: Stage actions consume centralized workflow state and product-owned services.

entries.reason_remaining: Filter and abstraction changes still enqueue legacy generation until workflow state and product generation services exist.
entries.removal_condition: Song updates enqueue product-owned services through centralized workflow state.

entries.reason_remaining: Regeneration stage comments still reference legacy stage names while the queue is being centralized.
entries.removal_condition: Regeneration stage documentation describes product-owned stages only.

entries.reason_remaining: Stub comments reference legacy full runners until product-owned render handlers replace them.
entries.removal_condition: Stub comments and handlers reference product-owned stage handlers only.

entries.reason_remaining: Staleness policy still references the legacy identity window constant as historical context.
entries.removal_condition: Staleness constants are product-owned and documented without referencing legacy script names.

entries.reason_remaining: Package documentation still reflects the legacy runner wrapper architecture.
entries.removal_condition: Pipeline package documentation describes product-owned service/adapters.

entries.reason_remaining: Progress parsing still understands legacy stdout while legacy runners remain inventoried.
entries.removal_condition: Progress is emitted by product-owned adapters with product-owned event contracts.

entries.reason_remaining: The frontend still receives imported asset paths that may point into POC-shaped output roots.
entries.removal_condition: Frontend receives product artifact URLs or product artifact paths only.
